description_long: This is a two-story commercial bakery building with a flat roof and a classic brick facade. The front features three large arched windows on the upper floor and three rectangular windows below, with a recessed entryway and large display windows at street level. The brickwork is accented by lighter stone or concrete trim around the windows and at the building's corners, giving it a traditional urban appearance. The side and rear walls are simpler, with fewer windows and a more utilitarian look, while the back includes a small red-and-white striped awning above a secondary entrance. The ground floor is designed for retail, likely with a bakery shopfront, while the upper floor may serve as storage or office space. The overall structure is robust, with masonry construction and minimal decorative elements beyond the brickwork and window arches.
description: A two-story brick bakery with arched upper windows, a recessed entry, and large street-level display windows. The facade features classic masonry details and a small striped awning at the rear.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance, three arched upper windows, three rectangular lower windows, and large display windows flanking the entry.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals four arched upper windows and a long, plain wall with minimal decoration and no visible entry.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain brick wall with two windows and a small red-and-white striped awning above a secondary door.
description_view_3: The right side view is mostly featureless, with solid brick and stonework and no windows or doors visible.
attribute_awning_with_bakery_branding: A small, semi-circular red-and-white striped awning is present above the rear entrance. There is no visible branding, logos, or text on the awning.
attribute_architectural_style: The building features a traditional urban commercial style with classic brickwork, arched windows, and stone trim, reminiscent of late 19th to early 20th-century American Main Street architecture.
